item.title: Markings that appear to show "86 47" seen on National Mall
item.description: A photographer from the Reuters news outlet saw the apparent "86 47" markings from atop the Washington Monument.

item.title: Trump taps prosecutor Jay Clayton as next director of national intelligence
item.description: Jay Clayton is currently the U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York.

item.title: World Cup opening ceremony kicks off in Mexico with Shakira
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup opening ceremony festivities got underway a little over an hour before the first kickoff in Mexico City's Estadio Azteca.

item.title: Trump taps federal prosecutor Jay Clayton for director of national intelligence role
item.description: President Trump on Thursday announced he's nominating former SEC chairman and current U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York Jay Clayton to be the next director of national intelligence. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: SpaceX IPO could see a "volatile few hours" after launch, market reporter says
item.description: SpaceX goes public on Friday, with its initial public offering poised to make waves. Joe Ciolli, executive editor of the First Trade Newsletter at Business Insider, shares his market performance predictions for Elon Musk's aerospace company.

item.title: U.S. charges 3 for alleged smuggling conspiracy involving unaccompanied minors
item.description: The Justice Department announced it has charged three people in Ohio for conspiring to smuggle unaccompanied minors across the U.S. border.

item.title: Trump says Iran strikes called off, citing progress on potential peace deal
item.description: President Trump said he has called off new military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the war.

item.title: The World Cup will draw millions of fans, but economic payoff is modest
item.description: Although the five-week soccer tournament starting on Thursday is the largest sporting event ever, the U.S. economic gains are likely to be muted.

item.title: Supreme Court hands down opinions on criminal trial venues, bankruptcy court, investment regulations
item.description: The Supreme Court issued new opinions in three court cases on Thursday. A series of major rulings remain as the end of the court's current term nears. CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford explains.

item.title: False alarm apparently triggered hazmat response at Pentagon, sources say
item.description: Multiple floors of the Pentagon were locked down for several hours Thursday morning and hazmat crews were deployed for what authorities had described as a "hazardous materials incident."

item.title: Is a high-yield savings account worth opening with inflation rising now?
item.description: Inflation is rising yet again, but is a high-yield savings account worth opening in response? Here's why it may be.

item.title: Should you delay veterinary care because of cost?
item.description: Rising veterinary costs are forcing pet owners to make tough decisions, but should delaying care be one of them?

item.title: Vance Boelter changes federal plea to guilty in lawmaker shootings
item.description: Vance Boelter pleaded guilty to the shootings of two Minnesota lawmakers and their spouses on Thursday as part of an agreement with federal prosecutors.

item.title: Trump says planned strikes on Iran are canceled, cites progress on potential deal
item.description: President Trump posted on social media Thursday that he's canceling planned strikes against Iran, citing progress on a potential peace deal. CBS News' Natalie Brand has more.

item.title: U.S. monitoring Chinese activity in South China Sea around disputed shoal
item.description: Analysts are scrutinizing recent Chinese maritime operations near Scarborough Shoal, an uninhabited but strategically located atoll near the Philippine island of Luzon, U.S. officials said.

item.title: Inflation is surging again. These 2 savings accounts will help you outpace it.
item.description: With inflation surging, savers should move their money into an account that can outpace it. Here are two to know now.

item.title: False alarm prompts hazmat response at Pentagon, sources say
item.description: A false alarm likely triggered a hazmat response at the Pentagon on Thursday, sources say. Employees are under a shelter in place order until an all clear is issued. CBS News' Eleanor Watson has the latest.

item.title: Top Trump officials announce criminal charges over migrant children allegedly smuggled
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche and Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mulln announced smuggling conspiracy charges linked to cases of unaccompanied migrant children brought into the U.S. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: Karmelo Anthony's family says "nobody wins" after murder verdict
item.description: Karmelo Anthony's mother Kala Hayes told CBS News that her son "didn't mean to hurt anyone" and "was defending himself" when he stabbed another student, Austin Metcalf.

item.title: U.S. cruise passengers accused of assaulting police in Bahamas
item.description: Four police officers were injured, including one who was taken to the hospital, authorities said.

item.title: Bill Gates grilled over Jeffrey Epstein ties
item.description: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates spent hours before members of the House Oversight Committee on Wednesday testifying about his ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. CBS News' Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: See when the U.S. men's soccer team is playing in 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team, which last appeared at the 2022 World Cup, will face Paraguay to kick off its 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Trump arch construction could take 20 hours a day for 2-3 years, documents say
item.description: The Interior Department is planning an aggressive work schedule to complete President Trump's arch near Arlington National Cemetery.

item.title: What to know about Iran's Kharg Island as Trump threatens to seize it
item.description: Kharg Island is a heavily fortified, strategically vital island off Iran's north coast that President Trump says the U.S. may seize control of militarily.

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off with Shakira at 1st of 3 opening ceremonies
item.description: With matches being played in 11 cities across the U.S., Mexico and Canada​, fans are getting three World Cup opening ceremonies.

item.title: How Boston Dynamics upgraded the Atlas robot
item.description: Atlas, a humanoid robot made by robotics company Boston Dynamics, has been upgraded from a version 60 Minutes saw in 2021, with joints that can fully rotate and hands that can grip a variety of objects.

item.title: Spanish soccer star Lamine Yamal: "The face of the new Spain"
item.description: As Spain's soccer prodigy steps into the global spotlight, he stays rooted in his multi-dimensional heritage.

item.title: Lamine Yamal's extended 60 Minutes interview in Spanish
item.description: Spanish soccer star Lamine Yamal sits down with 60 Minutes correspondent Jon Wertheim to talk about his love for soccer, the World Cup, and the pressure of playing on the world stage.

item.title: How one conversation reshaped Anderson Cooper's reporting
item.description: Anderson Cooper says his 2012 interview with Jordan Ghawi in Aurora, Colorado, forever changed how he covers mass shootings. Ghawi, whose sister Jessica was killed in the theater attack, asked journalists to say victims' names, not the shooter's. This week on 60 Minutes, Anderson Cooper visited the bedrooms of school shootings victims and spoke with the parents. The rooms have been left virtually untouched and have become memorials to young lives cut short.

item.title: A daughter's words inspire her parents to embrace life
item.description: Gracie Muehlberger, a 15-year-old student killed in the 2019 Saugus High School shooting, wrote a journal entry that inspired a mantra for her surviving parents Bryan and Cindy Muehlberger.
